name: Gary's Beef Stroganoff
recipeIngredient: 25 oz Red Wine, dry, full bodied
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and chop onions.
2. Slice mushrooms into large pieces (or use small whole mushrooms).
3. Dredge beef cubes in flour and lightly coat them.
4. Heat a large stewing pot with olive oil.
5. Brown beef cubes thoroughly in the olive oil.
6. Add onions, garlic, and mushrooms to the pot and sauté lightly.
7. Add red wine (all) and enough beef stock to barely cover the ingredients.
8. Add bay leaf (broken) and dill. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Stir in tomato paste.
10. Cover and simmer, stirring occasionally, for about 2 hours or until beef is very tender. The cooking liquid will thicken from the flour-coated beef.
11. If more liquid is needed during cooking, add additional beef stock sparingly.
12. Be careful to scrape the bottom of the pot when stirring to prevent flour from sticking and burning.
13. Just before serving, mix in 1 1/2 cups sour cream.
14. Transfer to a casserole dish or tureen and garnish with sprigs of fresh dill.
15. Serve with rice or buttered noodles.
